moduleDescription: Learn how advocacy becomes rights-based when it connects community voice, evidence, participation, and accountability.
lessonDescription: Introduces the difference between general advocacy and HRBA advocacy.
lessonPracticeFocus: Recognize how HRBA changes advocacy from general awareness raising into a rights-based accountability practice.
expectedOutput: Short learner response identifying two HRBA features in a CSO advocacy example.
blocksIncluded: Key Message; Text; Case Study; Accordion; Knowledge Check
buildStudioNotes: Use this lesson to anchor the course purpose and avoid long theory. Keep text concise and practical.

moduleDescription: Learn how advocacy becomes rights-based when it connects community voice, evidence, participation, and accountability.
lessonTitle: Rights Holders, Duty Bearers, and Power
lessonDescription: Clarifies rights holders, duty bearers, accountability relationships, and exclusion risks.
lessonPracticeFocus: Identify rights holders, duty bearers, and accountability relationships in a local CSO issue.
expectedOutput: Simple rights-holder and duty-bearer mapping note for one advocacy issue.
buildStudioNotes: Include clear examples and avoid abstract legal language. Use reflection to connect to the learner’s own organization.

moduleDescription: Practice moving from a community concern to a clear objective, evidence needs, and responsible stakeholder engagement.
lessonTitle: From Community Concern to Advocacy Objective
lessonDescription: Shows how to convert a problem into a realistic advocacy objective.
lessonPracticeFocus: Translate a broad community concern into a focused, safe, and rights-based advocacy objective.
expectedOutput: One focused advocacy objective based on a fictionalized or anonymized community concern.
buildStudioNotes: This lesson should include the strategic decision branching scenario. It should test judgment, not only recall.

moduleDescription: Practice moving from a community concern to a clear objective, evidence needs, and responsible stakeholder engagement.
lessonDescription: Introduces simple mapping of rights holders, duty bearers, allies, and risks.
lessonPracticeFocus: Use stakeholder and risk analysis to identify safe advocacy engagement options.
expectedOutput: Simple stakeholder and duty-bearer map with one risk note and one safe engagement step.
buildStudioNotes: Use resource and image blocks to support practical mapping. Knowledge check should test safe classification of stakeholders/risks.

moduleDescription: Prepare a clear advocacy message and a simple action outline that considers safety and follow-up.
lessonDescription: Explains how to shape a message around evidence, rights, and a practical ask.
lessonPracticeFocus: Draft a short advocacy message that uses credible non-sensitive evidence and a practical ask.
expectedOutput: Draft advocacy message using the structure: issue, evidence, rights concern, duty-bearer, and practical ask.
buildStudioNotes: Video may be an external or demo URL with transcript. Practical activity should be offline-friendly.

moduleDescription: Prepare a clear advocacy message and a simple action outline that considers safety and follow-up.
lessonDescription: Consolidates learning into a simple advocacy action outline.
lessonPracticeFocus: Bring together issue, rights holders, duty bearers, evidence, risks, message, and next step into a simple action outline.
expectedOutput: Completed HRBA advocacy action outline for a real or realistic CSO issue, using anonymized details.
blocksIncluded: Case Study; Reflection; Key Message; Resource
buildStudioNotes: This lesson should close the learning journey and route follow-up support. Do not create proof verification; keep as practice activity/resource.
